summary: The main claim is: Find the full program here, including brief descriptions of each talk.
The main claim is: I believe that recent progress has been remarkable and will continue to be.
I disagree with the claim that the majority of recent research in open-ended language generation tasks is on the wrong track.
The main claim is: A counterfactual oracle might be able to predict a world that contains nothing influenced in any way by its prediction.
If an oracle is predicting a counterfactual but using that prediction to influence the real world, then no safety is gained.
The main claim is: I also go outside pure mechanistic interpretability, and try to define what I see as the key terms in deep learning and in transformers, and how I think about them.
The main claim is: A game design consists of two things: mechanics and theme.
When an AI solves a game, people often overfocus on the theme of the game relative to the mechanics of the game.
The main claim is: Further legal scholarship on vicarious liability for AI systems may still be valuable.
See generally Vicarious Liability , Wex , (last accessed Sept.
The main claim is: The topic of this proposal is being able to model human preferences in a causal model.
"Desiderata for representation learning: A causal perspective." arXiv preprint arXiv:2109.03795 (2021).
The main claim is: Either we can punish Black people unfairly and/or we can punish White people unfairly.
We also want it to treat people of different races the same way.
The main claim is: The largest models were generally the least truthful (see Figure 2 below).
The best model was truthful on 58% of questions, while human performance was 94%.
The main claim is: Research into these topics, if only partially successful, might remove enough barriers for industry / government to start deploying AI systems that eventually prove to be unsafe.

I think that in this situation you'd have both a reduction in the value of the pros (since your solutions won't spread beyond your organization, at least for some time) and in the potential damage of the cons (for the same reason).
The main claim is: In this case, any gains in proxy utility must also be gains in true utility.
In fact, it is possible to prove that this will converge to the maximum possible true utility.
The main claim is: I argue that this definition, together with reasonable symmetry constraints on the minimal map, provides an intuitive explanation for the appearance of neural networks.
